reviews.review_text:
Good location
Comfortable cabin's
Foxtel on plus internet
On the bus route as well

This is our second stay here, its a nice quiet park and good for young kids. Its very basic, budget friendly and close to town.. the camp kitchen  has an oven microwave jug and fridge.  The games room is old and out dated and could definitely do with a revamp. It has 2 pool areas, 1 with a kids pool. The park backs onto the lake so comes with loads and loads of mozzies which honestly almost ruined the stay as they were relentless but cant blame the park for that.. its a short walk to MacDonald's and also forster mall which has kmart, cotton on, chemist,  coles, supercheap and other essential stores.. overall it is a nice place to stay, just expect basic and you wont be disappointed.. I would stay again. Pet friendly, kid friendly, caravan camping and cabin friendly.. no extras just relaxing in the outdoors.. and washing machines and dryers are $4 each wash and dry..

We stayed at Lanis Holiday Island for a short period. We had a small mishap with our carand caravan during our stay.  Josh and his team were very friendly and helpful to solve the problem. Would not hesitate to visit again when travelling through that part of the country.

Please do not stay here. The room is not clean and in much need of an upgrade,  the camp grounds are run down and not well kept.
To make matters worse, the permanent residents seemingly do not have to abide by the rules, having parties from 1am till 4am.
Great staff. Nice pool.

Did our laundry here during our stay and had our clothes stolen from the line. It made us feel on edge for the rest of our stay. No cctv to get it back. Would not recommend staying here or at least don’t hang your clothes out and keep your car locked at all times if you do
The place is not well maintained. We had to change the villa 3 times because of noise in AC and the fan. surprisingly staff did not know about it.

Up until now, service from the park and staff have been excellent. I recently booked accommodation for a trip to visit my Mother. On advice from my brother and sister, (Executor of Will) I cancelled my trip. I contacted the office and was told there would be a credit though no refund. My Mother passed away on Saturday before I was due to arrive and I won't be returning. Yes Lani's, a very busy time of year and you won't be out of pocket.

Absolutely would not stay here again.
No sheets throughout the cabin, you need to purchase your own or pay $15 for linen.
No towels provided, have to pay for those, no microwave safe bowls, only a pan, a pot, jug and toaster. The cabin was filthy. We said we needed to sweep before we left. And the receptionist so no it’s okay, that’s why we have house cleaners but it wasn’t done before we got there. So probably wasn’t cleaned 🙄
The pools are ok, bit murky. Kids loved it. That’s the main thing. They’ve blocked off the island so you can’t go out there. Half way down the road is blocked. Not worth the $500 for 3 nights stay that’s for sure.
Such a beautiful spot, friendly, happy staff, big sites, nice and quiet. Thanks for having us! We'll be back.

The neighbours across the road seemed to have 10 people in one house, all smoking like chimneys anecdotal drinking constantly. I had to keep my kids inside. They also parked in our driveway.

The grounds were constantly full of kids on e-scooters that kept almost running our kids over when we left the place, or popping out on front of cars. Nobody did anything about it

The internet/wifi didn't work at all. The booklet explained it might not work in all homes so i went to the location it was "guaranteed" and it did not work.

The TV didn't have the channels promised. I had a staff member investigate, he had a lot of excuses and said he was going to fix a bunch of the stations... no change

The kitchen didn't have sharp knives or a choping board. The pan had no lid.

The stove was so low that my baby kept trying to touch the hot plates Ababa turn it on. The gpo was located in a very hazardous location causing you to have to put your face to the hotplate to turn it off.

The cabin was fitted out with half finished or broken cupboards. The higher shelves were sprinkled with, what looked like, cockroache poo

There was one gpo available in the kitchen and if it was in use then the microwave couldn't open

The kettle leaked constantly

The taps were so small that you couldn't fit the kettle under to fill it up.

The bedroom door kept coming off its railings

We had one tiny soap for 4 people for 6 nights. And 4 coffee sachets.

Absolutely loved this place, nothing was to hard for the staff
...
Plenty for kids and adults too. 3 pools playgrounds. Plenty of open space. Facilities are clean and functional. Kids everywhere and enjoying themselves.
Sauberkeit könnte besser sein. Die Toiletten waren nach der Reinigung immer noch schmutzig.

Staff were friendly. Campground area was okay, some sites are dirt and others are grass. Our site wasn't flat so was challenging to set up. Facilities were okay, older but worked fine. Two pools. The midges were hardcore as your next to a creek so take loads of Deet and coils
Cabins comfortable Location is close to pubs clubs . Shopping centre. Staff always have a smile .

Overall was terrible, booked the pet friendly lodge for a few nights the lodge was dirty, protector sheets were stained the one sheet that came on the double bed was dirty, was eaten alive by bugs inside the cabin possible bed bugs,
Blankets were filthy and smelt like dog/ rotten washing.
Shower leaked throughout the bathroom and the sheet covering the lounge was filthy .
Had to go buy new stuff just to be able to fit comfortably
No proper table or chairs just a stand high bench with poxy bar stools which were not accessibly friendly for my mum who uses a walking aide

Just went up to Forster for the long weekend. Had been to Lanis before camping, but decided this time to stay in a cabin as wasn’t sure what the weather was going to do. Won’t be happening again.

Enter the room to find dead cockroaches, ants and moths all over the floor, bench tops and table. Cousin was staying in the room with the double bed whilst I was on the bunk bed. Wouldn’t call it a bunk bed as the bed was literally on the floor. Lifted up the mattress to put the sheet on to find even more dead bugs under the mattress. Wouldn’t call it a mattress either would call it a piece of foam. Had to pull the other mattress off the other bunk and double them up as the mattress is that thin your ass hits the floor. All dead bugs on the window tracks.
Double bed on wheels that all it does is roll around Everytime you roll over. Leaking taps, tiny tv in a stupid spot, rock hard couch that looks 30 years old, leaking taps.

Went to head office to speak with someone to get the old. Manager will be in tomorrow. Having read some reviews of people all complaining about the same thing thought I would also voice my opinion,
Has the potential to be a great park. Just needs to be tidied up with some new interiors.
Can’t call it a cabin to sleep 4 when an adult wouldn’t be able to climb into the top bunk, plus bottom bunk literally on the floor.

If a cabin is empty to for few days between bookings, maybe send cleaner again to double check everything. Not nice walking into dead critters everywhere. Plus get new beds with wheels with brakes on them. And get new bunks put in where you don’t have to get on your hands and knees to get in.

Will take my tent again next time. Disappointing

Was here for a romantic weekend away
Had  a great time
And lots to see locally
Especially the Beachs
Comfortable cabin which was value for money. The staff were very helpful and accommodating when I asked for a change on my booking. Great location. Close to food outlets etc.

One of the worst places we have ever stayed in. We booked and paid online paying $140 a night. We arrived and to our horror realised that there was no on suite (bathroom or toilet). We asked for an ‘upgrade’ and was charged an extra $10! Once in our ‘upgraded’ cabin with the basic facilities we again realised that there were no bed sheets or towels. We thought that perhaps they just forgot to provide them. When I contacted reception I was told that they can provide them for an extra $15!!! We have never heard anything more ridiculous in any of our extensive travels all over the world. To top it off the blanket provided had some complimentary black hair on it and the cabin was extremely uncomfortable. Our advice is stay far away from this place at all costs!
Cheap accommodation ok for a quick stop.
Central clean desent sized van sights
Very nice park, friendly staff good location close to everything also not bad fishing in the lagoon at the rear of the park as well as everywhere else in Forster
Great value for money

Amazing been coming to Lanis for almost 20 years. Each visit feels like a reunion with old friends, as the familiar faces and warm smiles greet me at the door. The atmosphere is always buzzing with laughter and good times. Over the years, I've created countless memories here, from celebratory dinners to quiet moments of reflection. It's a place that has truly become a second home, holding a special place in my heart.

Disappointing Experience - Would Not Recommend for Families with Young Kids

We booked a week-long stay over New Year's but ended up leaving a day early. Unfortunately, this place is not well-maintained, and there was a clear lack of management to enforce rules. We were staying with young children, and the constant presence of motorized scooters and bikes zooming by at high speeds was concerning. What's even more alarming is that not a single rider was wearing a helmet.

While I understand it's New Year's and people want to have fun, the noise levels were unbearable. The campsite repeatedly cranked up the music until 1-2 AM, and despite there being clear "quiet hours" in place, there was no one to address this. I had to go to the campers at 1 AM myself to ask them to turn the music down. That’s something the campground management should have handled.

Given that the campsite advertises quiet time starting at 10 PM, I expected more oversight. Unfortunately, this place felt more like a "free-for-all," with no one to enforce rules, especially for families with young children. I wouldn’t recommend this location for anyone staying with kids.

Pet friendly Villa was exactly as described, was clean and had everything we needed so suited our needs perfectly. Reception staff was courteous, we stayed midweek out of holiday season so park was pleasantly quiet, is close to shopping center for anything you need.
Unfortunately, Tonys Point Island is closed due to asbestos contamination, but it didn't inconvenience us, as with many coastal parks there is midges this time of year we just covered up or used repellent from dust till dawn when sitting outdoors.
Overall was nice place to stay when travelling with pets

* good location with lots of shade & closer to the town, but unfortunately no beach.  You have to drive out /or walk through the camping side on the main busy road to find coastal walking pathway to the town.
Suggestion: a coded (camp) gates to access existing beautiful walking track leading to town
* good services. I bit deteriorated old camping site. Unfortunately, nothing to rent regarding activities for adults (kids can rent small cars).
Suggestion:
- water pipes are shooting water as no nozzles on the pipes (thank you Matt! for organising one for us @ a camping site)
- availability/opportunity to rent a bycicle, boat/ kayak/ boards... and head off to cycling, fishing (or kayaking to town) from camping site.
* good staff, especially Matt (the gardener and handyman): never fake kind (which is a breeze 4 me) and helpful.
* great camping spot: we were lucky as we had many opportunities to choose (before school holidays). Had our own direct drinking water access nearby & a bin & clean toilets (thank you Matt 😊

We arrived after hours and despite speaking to them via email and phone several times can you imagine our dismay to find we had no bed linen or towels and forget the shower gel, apparently if you read the obscure fine print they do tell you but not in anything we received, you can get these as an added extra but that's no help if you arrive after hours. That  predicament was solved by a quick trip to Kmart at 8am next morning because we also had to buy pillows because the 2 we got on our bed were useless

We booked an ensuite site and couldn't ask for anything better. Great spot with water views. The perfect set up for our caravan. Cement slab to the ensuite door, clean shower, toilet, and vanity with sink - lockable door allows you to keep your belongings safe and makes for a very handy weekly stay. We are return patrons to Lanis and have tried almost every site booking possible from cabins, to the powered sites, and even stayed out on the island. We love it every time.

Recently stayed in one of the family cabins, due to attending a weekend sporting event.
Upon arrival it's evident that the location is ideal, we had a water front cabin,..
Once inside our cabin we noticed a large amount of black hair, all over the floor throughout the entire cabin.
Whilst making my sons bed, I unfold the additional blanket provided,  and again,  the whole blanket, both sides covered in black hair..
Thinking it was only the 1 blanket, I grabbed another from the other bunk bed, only to find that too covered with black hair, i unfolded every single blanket provided,  and all were covered in black hair...Thankfully we brought our own bedding as we always do..
The bathroom,  while it was clean,  every single towel,  had black hair in it, the bathroom floor,  black hair all over it.
As we were only there for 2 nights,  we gritted our teeth and beared with it.. But please please please,  have your staff carefully checked the linen before providing it to the cabins. it's not pleasant knowing you are sleeping in someone else's DNA.
We are booked to stay there again in the coming weeks, so please thoroughly check linen you are providing, because I will very closely..

If you’re thinking of staying here DONT! This was the most terrible stay that I’ve had at foster by far. The bed sheets were so dirty and I even think there was a bug crawling on me in the middle of the night. The blankets were so it itchy. Probably because of the dust all over them making me sneeze all night. And to make it worse I’m asthmatic.

Terrific management service, good spot.
Thanks for your assistance, Josh
Great place to stay clean and tidy
Cute and Cosy Hut, close to Bowling Club and Shopping centre.  Beautiful location ❤️❤️

Accommodation desperately needs updating and maintenance. Taps/shower dripping all night, partner decided to sleep in bunks due to main bed mattress rolling into centre.  Website not clearly worded that linen was not provided at all, not just for bunks. Location was good.

Super impressed with this accomodation! I stayed here during my first time in Forster on a solo road trip and was was pleasantly surprised with my little cabin. I particularly enjoyed having my own cooking facilities and although I was initially a little nervous about the communal amenities the showers were also amazing! Could not ask for anything more!
A nice place to visit and have great fun. Mosquitoes 🦟 are quite annoying but overall experience is good

We had the most amazing time away with our kids. The staff were great nothing was too big of an issue!
We didn’t read the reviews before booking after we booked we did have a read of the ones & I’m sooo glad we didn’t read them before booking. They were negative reviews but after going ahead with our booking we are sooo glad we did! I could not fault the place at all, AND the best water pressure & HOT showers!
Overall a wonderful place to take the kiddies & have a relaxing weekend!
The staff who worked the weekend of the 19-21st you were absolutely amazing & we appreciate how kind welcoming & warm you were! ✨

We booked a pet friendly accommodation with other friends in the end we changed our minds and we called up 2 weeks prior to Easter getaway to change it and they were accomodating to give us a different rooms. As we haven’t been there before unfortunately when we got there  the place we booked / changed (we even had to pay more ) was really old and run down unfortunately we couldn’t stay there it had a bad smell.

Our friends who stayed there in the pet friend accommodation had much newer room with much better facilities.
Cabins are clean and comfortable.  Great location.   It would be great if there were more for families,  entertainment or activities for kids would be ideal.

Two of us stayed at Lanis Holiday Island whilst on a coastal road trip. We opted for an unpowered site. The plots of land were large, however the mosquitos were a nuisance during our stay, so come prepared with mosquito repellent if you plan on being outside at nighttime.

The amenities at Lanis seem to be overdue for an update. Nevertheless, the staff are hard-working and diligent, and I noted this during our stay at Lanis. Thank you!

We have been coming to Lanis for over 15 years, I just want to give a big shout out to all the staff who have always been super friendly and helpful. Nothing is ever a problem and they go above and beyond to make our stay as enjoyable as possible.
Excellent can't wait to visit again
It was a nice place to stay, but a little run down. We camped and the mozzies were pretty bad due to the water nearby.

We were towing a campertrailer on our way south, but due to the rain we decided instead on a cabin for an overnight stay.  The cottage was budget  (which is what we wanted) and all we needed but we were super impressed with the service of the lady at the front counter on arrival and upon leaving.  She greeted us with a smile, was helpful, attentive and interested. Great Old fashioned customer service.
A great location too.  We shall be back for a longer stay in the camper trailer in future!

Short notice booking following see a friend go there and the photos of location and facilities.  Lanus holiday Island didn't disapoint,  stayed in a nice lodge,  walked to pub, checked out camping grounds, boat ramp and park facilities.  Staff very friendly check in and out easy, clean and good value accommodation.
Very unclean

If I could leave 0 Stars I would.||The management were rude and unprofessional. They were inflexible and not helpful at all.||The park is dated and left in disrepair, not to mention the amenities are falling apart and filthy.|I went to use the bathroom in a hurry and was unable to find any bathroom doors with locks on them, or a toilet without staining. I was worried to use the shower as they were stained and full of mould.||The park staff don't even care to clean the park of general rubbish, you can see it piled up in front of long term resident's caravans, as well as within the amenities. The smell travels throughout the park.||Safety in the park does not seem to be a worry for the management as we saw visitors speeding through the park, some who appeared to be driving under the influence and young people appearing too young to have a licence, driving the park's buggies throughout the park without any helmets.||I won't be going back and I wouldn't recommend anyone go holiday here

Wouldn’t bother with this place as they have no care in the world for their customers. Spoke to the locals living in the park and they had nothing but bad reviews about the management and the way they are being treated. The amenities are disgusting and need to be updated. Definitely wouldn’t take kids there

We camped on the bushland side and it was excellent. The lake was beautiful and warm, the toilets were really clean. We felt really secluded. Great location. We drove up the main park for showers and pool facilities and was really clean.

Upon arrival we checked in. I looked over the room and the mattresses in the kids bunk room were stained, I then looked at the top bunk and there were 2 ant nests and ants crawling on the walls of the cabin/ bunk bed. Spider webs and spiders on the quilt for the bed! I called reception straight away they sent a cleaner down and the replaced the mattresses and sprayed a fly spray to kill the ants, she had another male worker with her who said " why cant they all just sleep in the other room" suggesting a family of 5 sleep in a queen bed! I asked to be moved however at the time they couldn't accommodate that. We came back after the issue was sorted and there were ants all dead on a clean bed! I called and requested for the manager to come down.I told the owners of the workers comment for the owner to justify and shrug it off like it was ok because that's just the type of guy he is. Just goes to show the owners lack of care of our situation. The husband and wife who own the park had a looked and said it was just the time of year and the ants are bad due to the weather. They asked us what we would like to do to resolve the situation. We requested for a discount. They later called with a cabin that become available. We checked it out and it was much better. No compensation at all for the muck around! They had a family in our original cabin the following day!
The facilities need an update for sure! Cabins are old and run down. The games room wasn't much of a games room. The cinema room was great. Pools are ok however they were murky in colour everyday which was off putting.
Alot of people letting their children swim unsupervised, people speeding on the roads in the park, I couldn't let my children ride freely as I witnessed many times people driving/ speeding not paying attention. Fireworks being let off in the park right behind our cabin. All round chaos! Safe to say ill never stay there again!

During our stay there were police who came in to patrol the park! That was something I've never experienced staying in a family holiday park.

It was disappointing as I booked for a group of 3 family's and they weren't happy with the accommodation or facilities either.
Great

Stayed here for only one night, but won’t hesitate returning. We stayed on an unpowered site - was very peaceful and close to amenities. Amenities were relatively clean and the pool was a nice, unexpected bonus.

It’s a little way out of town - you’d have to drive to get anywhere, but that wasn’t much of a concern.

Forster is a lovely spot - we’ll be back… hopefully for longer next time!

This place is beautiful!!!! my family and i stayed here for 3 nights and 4 days, and it was soo enjoyable :) they provide free wifi and the ppl working regularly cut the trees so that random branches dont fall on your tent/caravan! its also really close to mcdonalds and hungry jacks which is the best part.
Theres also 2 pools - one of them is filled with ducks who POO everywhere so i dont reccommend that one but the other one is nice. there is also a rule where everyone has to be quiet after 10pm to let people have their peace and quiet 😁
also i was playing volleyball with my mum and these group of kids ran by and cheered me on it was very wholesome AND i was playing and my ball ran off and this nice old couple returned it to me and we became friends :) i didnt visit many parts of the campsite but for what i saw, this place is amazingggggggggggggg
Good
Awesome room and cabin happy with  that

We arrived late to Lani’s they had everything there that we needed to access the park and find our site plus all sorts of other info. We had a powered caravan site great site plenty of room. The annex pad is heavy duty shade cloth it is great at stopping send and leaves from getting into the van. Amenities were clean. It is located close to all conveniences with very little road noise. Park owners/managers were very friendly and helpful. We will stay there again.

Unfortunately had a terrible experience with this park. The manager was sarcastic and rude as were the office employees, there were illegally allowed fire works and underage drinking and fighting and the place stunk from all the trash sitting around.
There were kids and young people getting abused, no safety and charged us more than what we were told it was going to be.
Toilets and pool was disgusting and dirty, the only decent thing at this run down hell hole full of yobos was the lovely lady at the cafe! Even my kids felt unsafe while we were there. People who live at the park are constantly yelling at the kids, the police do walk through regularly- people have their possessions stolen, it’s a nightmare and the reports from people in the area are even worse then anything I’ve witnessed.
We went for a walk in the neighbouring park, it was the opposite- dont go for cheaper, in this case it definitely ruined our get away.
Lanis Island camping was great. Wide open spaces and good facilities. We'll be back :)
Loved visiting there,  it was lovely, friendly staff and great service, and friendly visitors as well, highly recommend this lovely holiday park.

had to stay there for an operation in Foster would be out of hospital by 11 or twelve they wanted to charge 152 dollars for extra hour or two,so my daughter and wife had to sleep in the car outside of the hospital from 7 in the morning till eleven

Very clean facilities!
Friendly staff 👍👍

We stayed at the campground for 3 nights and could not have asked for a better experience. The location is incredible, I had the best shower I’ve ever had and the amenities were so clean and in good condition! I would highly recommend this place to anyone looking for a great camping spot
Nice park, starting to age but clean and comfortable.

Good fun place especially when travelling with kids sports team all the kids were able to hang out till late hours just having fun exploring and being kids plus parents could enjoy a nice BBQ together and have a good laugh in a friendly environment. Really good quality.

This was one of the most relaxing holidays I have had. We took our caravan and it was so easy to maneuver around the park. The staff are incredibly friendly and helpful nothing is a problem. There is plenty to do as well. I will definitely be back.
Staff and owners are extremely arrogant
Ticked all the boxes. Very clean.
Favourite place to camp when in town. Close to everything the area has to offer.

Terrible customer service, unclean rooms.
I had been a returning customer at this park, over the years and the care and customer service has gone down hill. The last time I booked here I ended up having a serious back injury requiring surgery and was unable to go on the holiday. I gave them just under a weeks notice, even providing specialist letters to confirm my injury, pain and the fact that I could not travel. After months of being brushed off and told I’d be contacted by management I still have no money at all returned to me- with them keeping the entire $1400. All I requested was a credit so that we could visit at a later date but the staff were repeatedly rude and just kept brushing me off- never giving me a straight answer. It’s a shame as this park could be great, but as I have heard off many others I know that used to stay here the staff and customer services are turning people off staying here.

Lani’s Holiday Island in Forster NSW was very disappointing especially being a G’Day parks member. The reception was friendly but we were given a caravan site at the back of the park which was spacious enough only to find that there was no power pole to plug into so had to move to another vacant site. The amenity block for the 10 sites consisted of 2 toilets and 1 small shower per Gents & Ladies. These were decorated with spider webs and bugs, plus no hot water, soap or paper towels for hand washing. The shower did have hot water and good pressure. The whole park has a neglected feel about it and were glad to only stay for 1 night for $50 (did not receive membership price) Location is ok, not far from Stocklands Shopping Centre and close to the water.
Great little place to stay, backs onto the river fun for the kids and pet friendly
Old, but the cabin was clean and the staff were great
This is an Awsome caravan park in a gorgeous location
Extremely rude and unfriendly
Right on the water with great facilities and friendly staff.
A great family orientated caravan park, with cabins.
